title: Intense Turkish Chickpeas Pide
description: A delicious, yeasty, and tender Turkish dish made with chickpeas, perfect for a crowd-pleaser. This flavorful pide is high in protein and features a crispy crust.

ingredients.name: Active Dry Yeast
ingredients.notes: Instant yeast can be used as a substitute

ingredients.name: All-Purpose Flour
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with gluten-free flour

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the yeast and warm water. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until frothy.
Add the flour, salt, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the bowl. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat a paella pan or a skillet over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on the desired size of your pide.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Brush the dough with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil and sprinkle with chopped onion, minced garlic, cumin, and paprika.
Transfer the dough to the preheated pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
In a separate pan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chickpeas and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Spread the cooked chickpeas over the cooked pide, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Return the pide to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
